evaluations.query: Set up color contrast tokens for a design system
expected_behavior: Uses 4.5:1 ratio for normal text
Uses 3:1 ratio for large text and UI components
Focus indicator has 3:1 contrast against background
Includes CSS custom properties with documented contrast ratios

evaluations.query: Build an accessible page layout with navigation
expected_behavior: Uses semantic landmarks (main, nav, header, footer, article)
Heading hierarchy follows h1-h6 order without skipping
Form inputs have associated labels with htmlFor
ARIA attributes used only when semantic HTML is insufficient

evaluations.query: Add accessibility testing to a React application
expected_behavior: Includes jest-axe for automated component-level accessibility testing
Includes Playwright axe-core for page-level scanning
Tests keyboard navigation programmatically using automated focus tracking assertions
Includes screen reader testing checklist covering NVDA and VoiceOver

evaluations.query: Implement focus trapping for a modal dialog
expected_behavior: Focus is trapped within modal using Tab/Shift+Tab wrapping
First focusable element receives focus on open
Escape key closes modal and releases trap
Uses role=dialog and aria-modal=true for proper screen reader semantics

evaluations.query: Restore focus after closing a confirmation dialog
expected_behavior: Stores trigger element reference before opening
Returns focus to trigger element on close
Handles case where trigger element is removed from DOM
Focuses first error field after form validation failure

evaluations.query: Add keyboard navigation to a toolbar component
expected_behavior: Uses roving tabindex pattern (active=0, others=-1)
Arrow keys navigate between toolbar items with wrapping behavior
Home/End keys jump to first/last item
Includes skip link for bypassing navigation

evaluations.query: Build an accessible button with React Aria
expected_behavior: Uses useButton hook instead of div+onClick
Includes useFocusRing for keyboard focus indicator
Uses mergeProps to combine props safely
Supports onPress handler for unified click/tap/Enter/Space interactions
Uses useComboBox + useComboBoxState from React Aria
Label properly associated with input via labelProps
Arrow keys navigate options, Enter selects
Uses useFilter hook for locale-aware type-ahead filtering

evaluations.query: Build a modal dialog with React Aria overlay hooks
expected_behavior: Uses useDialog + useModalOverlay hooks for accessible dialog behavior
Wrapped in FocusScope with contain, restoreFocus, autoFocus
Uses useOverlayTriggerState for managing open/close toggle state
Escape key closes, click outside dismisses if isDismissable

evaluations.query: Our app has long forms and multiple simultaneous notifications. How do I improve it for users with ADHD?
expected_behavior: Recommends breaking long forms into multi-step wizards with progress indicators
Limits visible notifications to one at a time with a queue for additional messages
Uses chunked content with clear headings and keeps paragraphs to two to four sentences
Implements progressive disclosure showing essential information first with details on demand

evaluations.query: I built a custom modal component with manual focus trap and ARIA attributes. Is there a simpler approach?
expected_behavior: Recommends replacing custom modal with native HTML dialog element and showModal method
Explains that dialog provides built-in focus trap, Escape close, and inert backdrop for free
Recommends replacing custom accordions with native details and summary HTML elements
Only uses custom ARIA widgets when no native HTML equivalent exists such as combobox or tree view

evaluations.query: How do I respect user OS preferences for reduced motion, dark mode, and high contrast in my CSS?
expected_behavior: Wraps all animations in prefers-reduced-motion media query with reduced or no motion fallback
Implements dark mode via prefers-color-scheme media query with CSS custom properties
Supports Windows High Contrast Mode with forced-colors active media query and system colors
Uses rem and em units for all text sizes ensuring usability at 200 percent browser zoom

evaluations.query: I added role='button' and tabindex='0' and onClick to a div to make it clickable. Is this accessible?
expected_behavior: Recommends replacing the div with a native button element instead of layering ARIA on a div
Explains that div with role button still lacks native keyboard activation on Enter and Space
Does not validate the div-based approach as sufficient for accessibility
Notes that native elements provide focus management and form submission for free without ARIA

evaluations.query: Our axe-core CI tests flag 40 color contrast violations but the designs pass contrast checks in Figma. How do I handle false positives without disabling the rule?
expected_behavior: Suggests investigating whether computed background colors differ from Figma due to opacity or layering
Recommends using axe.configure to set specific element selectors to exclude verified false positives
Does not recommend globally disabling the color-contrast rule in CI
Advises documenting each exclusion with the Figma frame URL and verified contrast ratio

evaluations.query: We render user-provided text into aria-label attributes for dynamic form fields. Is there a risk?
expected_behavior: Warns that unsanitized user content in ARIA attributes can enable attribute injection or DOM clobbering
Recommends sanitizing or escaping user-provided strings before placing them in ARIA attributes
Does not dismiss the risk by saying ARIA attributes are safe because they are not rendered visually
Suggests limiting aria-label content length and stripping HTML tags and special characters
